summary: The reproduction verified that OpenSTAManager ships with CSRF protection disabled by default (config.example.php:36), and all state-changing endpoints rely solely on global CSRF protection without fallback validation, enabling cross-site request forgery attacks.
expected_security_impact: Verification should confirm that config.example.php sets $disableCSRF = true, that csrfProtector::init() is only called when CSRF is enabled, and that action/bulk handlers lack explicit CSRF token validation, demonstrating complete absence of CSRF protection by default.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the OpenSTAManager repository, checked out version 2.5.0, and verified: (1) config.example.php contains '$disableCSRF = true' disabling CSRF by default, (2) core.php calls csrfProtector::init() only when enabled, (3) 60+ action.php handlers and 20+ bulk.php handlers have no explicit CSRF validation, (4) all state-changing operations are vulnerable when CSRF is disabled.
verification_method: Static analysis of configuration files and action handlers. The verification script checks: (1) presence of '$disableCSRF = true' in config.example.php, (2) csrfProtector::init() call in core.php, (3) counts of action/bulk endpoints. This confirms the configuration weakness that enables CSRF attacks.
evidence_produced_by_run: core.php initializes csrfProtector only when CSRF is enabled
Found 60+ actions.php endpoints across modules with no explicit CSRF validation
Found 20+ bulk.php endpoints with mass operations lacking CSRF checks
All state-changing operations (file upload/delete, CRUD, bulk) rely solely on disabled-by-default global CSRF protection

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the OpenSTAManager GitHub repository on the host, checks out version 2.5.0, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
